name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Peanut Butter Cookie Crust and set aside.
2. Combine chocolate chips, 1 tbsp light corn syrup, and 1 tbsp water in a double boiler. Cook over simmering water until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth. Remove from heat and cool well.
3. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine peanut butter, 1/3 cup sugar, 3 tbsp light corn syrup, and 3 tbsp water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until sugar dissolves and mixture is well blended. Pour into a bowl and stir in peanuts; cool.
4. Beat egg whites with an electric mixer on high speed until foamy. Gradually add 2 tbsp sugar, 1 tbsp at a time, beating well after each addition. Add vanilla and continue beating until stiff, glossy peaks form when beaters are lifted slowly.
5. Fold egg white mixture into whipped cream, then fold in peanut butter mixture. Pour half of peanut butter mixture onto the cookie crust. Drizzle half of chocolate mixture over filling. Top with remaining filling and drizzle remaining chocolate in parallel lines over filling. Use a knife to cut across lines at 1-inch intervals.
6. Freeze until firm. Wrap securely in aluminum foil. Return to freezer and continue freezing for 8 hours or overnight.
7. Remove from freezer 10 minutes before serving. Crush cookies and melt butter or margarine. Combine them and mix well. Press crumb mixture into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch pie plate. Chill until set.
